prompt:
Robert: Claudia, you've always been the idealist. Let's be practical for once.
Claudia: Practicality, according to you, means bulldozing everything in sight.
Robert: It's called progress, Claudia. The world moves on whether you approve or not.
Claudia: And I suppose the people who live in those homes are just casualties of progress?
Robert: They'll be compensated. Generously.

How likely is each emotion Claudia is feeling at the end of this exchange?

Ellie: You're not seriously going to present your slides in that format, are you?
Gabe: What's wrong with my slides?
Ellie: Nothing, if you want the board to fall asleep by slide four.
Gabe: I think the board can handle a few paragraphs of text, Ellie.
Ellie: Sure. And I think you can handle me presenting in your place. Shall we settle this at the meeting?

How likely is each emotion Gabe is feeling?

Brenda: Tessa, I saw your grades. We need to talk.
Tessa: Mom, I don't want to do this right now.
Brenda: You've been saying that for weeks. When exactly is the right time?
Tessa: How about never? How about you stop reading my mail?
Brenda: When you live in this house under my roof, your mail is my business.
Tessa: Fine. I'll make sure the next letter has my moveout date on it.

How likely is each emotion Brenda is feeling after Tessa's last line?

Sean: Emma, I've been meaning to tell you something for a while.
Emma: Okay...
Sean: I've been seeing someone. For about six months.
Emma: Six months? Sean, we just had dinner with my parents last weekend.
Sean: I know. I'm sorry. I didn't know how to tell you.
Emma: Then why are you telling me now? In our kitchen, on a Tuesday?
Sean: Because I'm moving in with her. Next month.

How likely is each emotion Emma is feeling?

Felix: Vanessa, please, just give me five minutes.
Vanessa: You had five months. You could have picked up the phone any day.
Felix: I know. I was scared. That's not an excuse, it's just what happened.
Vanessa: Scared of what, Felix? Of hearing me say I was tired?
Felix: Of hearing you say we were over.
Vanessa: We've been over for three of those five months. You just didn't want to know.

How likely is each emotion Felix is feeling after Vanessa's final line?
